name: Grade 11 Skateboarding & Cycling — Semester 2
description: An 18-week Grade 11 pathway for independent research, evaluation, and sophisticated disciplinary communication through Skateboarding & Cycling. Learning advances through inquiry, practice, authentic application, revision, and communication.
learningGoals: Develop grade 11 knowledge and transferable skills in independent research, evaluation, and sophisticated disciplinary communication through Skateboarding & Cycling.
Use appropriate evidence, vocabulary, tools, and methods to explain reasoning.
Plan, revise, collaborate responsibly, and communicate a well-supported conclusion.

lessons.title: Launch inquiry: independent research, evaluation, and sophisticated disciplinary communication through Skateboarding & Cycling
lessons.objective: I can develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about independent research, evaluation, and sophisticated disciplinary communication through Skateboarding & Cycling and explain my reasoning with appropriate evidence.

activities.title: Question and evidence
activities.learnerAction: The learner develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about independent research, evaluation, and sophisticated disciplinary communication through Skateboarding & Cycling, documents evidence in an age-appropriate format, and follows applicable safety, ethics, and collaboration expectations.

successCriteria: The learner completes a grade 11 task connected to independent research, evaluation, and sophisticated disciplinary communication through Skateboarding & Cycling.

The learner revises, seeks feedback, or identifies a specific next step.
